reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21: YOLOS signature detection proven FIRST in headless Chrome (transformers.js object-detection, WASM). The int8/q8 builds fail on WASM (ConvInteger), so the float q4f16 build is used — it locates the drawn signature in both sample documents (contract 0.84, letter 0.72). The deployed page was then driven end-to-end (validate-signature-detection.mjs). Samples are first-party procedurally-generated mock documents with drawn pen-stroke signatures; ledgered as archetype procedural.

rubric.evidence: Correctness verified in headless Chrome (transformers.js object-detection, WASM) BEFORE building: the YOLOS signature detector locates the handwritten signature in both sample documents with a plausible box + score (contract signature:0.84, letter signature:0.72 on q4f16). The deployed page (scripts/validate-signature-detection.mjs) reproduces this — clicking a sample runs a real forward pass and draws the box. The box + score are the model's real output, not canned.
rubric.notes: DISTINCT from every built object detector: DETR/YOLO/RT-DETR/D-FINE/YOLOS-COCO detect general objects, face-detector finds faces, CRAFT/scene-text find text regions, table-transformer finds tables — this one locates SIGNATURES, a capability slot with no built counterpart, exactly the way table-transformer occupies the tables slot.

rubric.evidence: Input a document image → an array of detections, each a box (xmin/ymin/xmax/ymax in original pixels) + a confidence for the single class 'signature'. The page scales the boxes to the displayed image and overlays them. This is the standard object-detection I/O, applied to a distinct target.
rubric.notes: percentage:false so boxes are pixel coords; the overlay scales by clientWidth/naturalWidth.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (onnx-community/yolos-base-signature-detection-ONNX, linked), task (object-detection), the single class, architecture (YOLOS — DETR-style, ViT backbone), quantisation (q4f16, with an honest note that the int8 build won't run on WASM because of ConvInteger), backend (WASM), download (~210 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The How-it-works block shows the real pipeline call. No invented API.
rubric.notes: The worker imports the shared loadPipeline — no bespoke loading path.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnx-community/yolos-base-signature-detection-ONNX', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q4f16', sizeMB:207}). First visit shows an explicit Download (~210 MB); the ONNX is cached by transformers.js so a returning visit auto-initialises (checking -> ready) with no Download click. Verified: fresh profile showed Download; clicking it enabled Choose image.
rubric.notes: Standard pipeline caching under the repo path; auto-init on return holds via the shared loader.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (~210 MB) -> ready (Choose image enabled) -> scanning -> verdict + boxed overlay + readout. Detection failures surface on the page in a role=status .err region, not only the console. Verified download -> ready -> detect end-to-end on both samples.
rubric.notes: A 'No signature found.' verdict is shown when nothing clears the threshold, so an empty result is never silent.

rubric.evidence: Real controls: drag-and-drop a document, a Choose image file picker, and two sample-document thumbnails that run detection. Every control is labelled and keyboard-operable; no dead controls. The threshold is fixed at a sensible 0.3.
rubric.notes: Uploaded documents are read locally (FileReader data URL) and never leave the tab.

rubric.evidence: The boxes + scores are the model's real detections; the readout shows the live backend + latency + count. The page is HONEST that it detects the PRESENCE and LOCATION of a signature only — it does not read the name, verify who signed, or check authenticity — and that it expects real pen-stroke marks (the samples use drawn signatures), so faint marks or unusual layouts can be missed; the confidence is shown so the user can judge.
rubric.notes: Samples are first-party, procedurally generated mock documents (contract + letter with a drawn pen-stroke signature, no third-party imagery, no real person), ledgered in image-provenance/ledger.json as archetype 'procedural' by content hash (07247e59… / cd3b13ab…); their detections were verified, not asserted.

rubric.evidence: The verdict and status are aria-live; controls have visible labels and focus styles; the file picker is a real input. The overlay boxes are decorative (pointer-events:none) and the verdict states the count as text, so the result does not rely on the visual box alone.
rubric.notes: The pink box colour is redundant to the textual verdict + count.

rubric.evidence: Verified with no horizontal overflow at desktop (1280px) and mobile (360px) in the end-to-end run (scripts/validate-signature-detection.mjs). The document stage uses max-width:100% so the scanned image and its box overlay scale to the viewport; the overlay boxes are recomputed from clientWidth on load + resize so they stay aligned when the image reflows.
rubric.notes: The sample thumbnails and tools wrap on narrow screens; the readout is a flex-wrap row.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker so the UI stays responsive; a single detection forward pass on a document image is fast on WASM. The ~210 MB download is one-time (cached).
rubric.notes: One forward pass yields every box — no autoregression.

guidanceConsulted.query: run object detection in a worker and overlay boxes without blocking the page
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep the main thread free for rendering the overlay.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — detection runs in a module Worker; the main thread only scales + draws the returned boxes.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: the page stays responsive during a scan

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible result that does not rely on a coloured box alone
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic results in aria-live and provide the outcome as text, not only a visual marker.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the verdict ('N signatures found') and status are aria-live text; the box overlay is redundant decoration.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: the result is readable without seeing the box

guidanceConsulted.query: process a user-supplied document image locally without uploading it
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep sensitive user files on-device; read them with FileReader rather than sending to a server.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — dropped/chosen documents are read as a local data URL and inferred in-tab; nothing is uploaded.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: no network request carries the document

openQuestions: Would adding a 'crop the signature' action (using the detected box) make the redaction/e-signature use case more concrete?
The detector expects pen-stroke marks; is there a cleanly-licensed model that also handles typed/e-signature stamps?
followUpGoals: Add a redact/crop action that uses the detected box to blur or extract the signature region.
Add a batch mode: drop many pages and flag which are unsigned.
Explore a signature-verification (same-signer) model as a distinct follow-on family, not a re-skin of this detector.
summary: Signature detection (YOLOS) — a distinct vision family: it LOCATES handwritten signatures in a document, a capability slot with no built counterpart (the COCO detectors find general objects, table-transformer finds tables, CRAFT finds text). Proven in the browser on transformers.js WASM — the int8 build fails on WASM (ConvInteger), so the float q4f16 build (~210 MB, Apache-2.0) is used and detects the signature in both first-party sample documents (contract 0.84, letter 0.72). Drop/choose a document or click a sample; the real detection is boxed on the image with a verdict + readout. Honest that it finds presence/location only — not identity or authenticity — and expects real pen strokes. Crop/redact, batch triage, and a distinct verification family are recorded follow-ups.
